title: lupinus perennis subsp. latifolius
intro: Lupinus perennis subsp. latifolius, also known as the narrow-leaved lupine, is a subspecies of perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the lupin family. It is native to North America and has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The narrow-leaved lupine has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Lupinus perennis subsp. latifolius, such as teas and infusions, can be used to reap its health benefits, and it has also been used as a natural remedy for various ailments.
